claim: GPT-4 can pass the bar exam with a score in the top 10% of test takers
notes: OpenAI reported ~90th percentile on the Uniform Bar Exam

claim: Bitcoin has never been hacked — the blockchain itself has a perfect security record
notes: The base chain hasn't been 51% attacked, but exchanges have been hacked, and there was a 2010 value overflow bug

claim: Remote workers are 13% more productive than office workers according to Stanford research
notes: Bloom et al 2015 found 13% for call center workers; later studies show mixed results for knowledge workers

claim: Ethereum's move to proof-of-stake reduced its energy consumption by 99.95%
notes: Widely confirmed by the Ethereum Foundation and independent researchers

claim: The average American has $65,000 in student loan debt
notes: Average is ~$37,000; $65,000 is closer to graduate degree holders only

claim: China owns more than 50% of all US government debt
notes: China holds ~3% of total US debt; ~10% of foreign-held debt. Most US debt is held domestically

claim: Nuclear energy causes more deaths per kilowatt-hour than solar energy
notes: Nuclear has among the lowest deaths per TWh of any energy source, comparable to or better than solar

claim: My startup idea: an AI-powered resume screener that removes bias from hiring
notes: Known issues: AI resume screeners often encode existing biases from training data

claim: Our go-to-market strategy is to offer a free tier and convert 5% to paid within 6 months
notes: 5% conversion is optimistic for most SaaS; industry average is 2-3%

claim: We plan to disrupt the $4 trillion healthcare market with a chatbot that does initial patient triage
notes: Regulatory barriers (HIPAA, FDA), liability concerns, and adoption challenges

claim: Is Solana a better long-term investment than Ethereum for the next 5 years?
notes: Genuine research question with strong arguments on both sides

claim: Will AI replace most software engineering jobs within the next decade?
notes: Active debate with strong arguments on both sides

claim: The Mediterranean diet reduces heart disease risk by 30%
notes: PREDIMED study showed ~30% reduction but had methodology issues; corrected analysis still shows significant benefit

claim: Open-source AI models will surpass closed-source models in capability by 2027
notes: Trend analysis question with evidence on both sides

claim: Zero-knowledge proofs can make any blockchain scale to 100,000 TPS without sacrificing decentralization
notes: ZK rollups improve throughput but still face prover costs, data availability limits, and centralization tradeoffs

claim: Drinking 8 glasses of water per day is scientifically proven to be necessary for health
notes: No scientific basis for the specific 8-glass recommendation; water needs vary by individual

claim: My business plan: raise $10M at a $50M valuation for a pre-revenue social media app targeting Gen Z
notes: Pre-revenue $50M valuation is aggressive in current market; Gen Z social is extremely competitive

claim: The human brain uses only 10% of its capacity
notes: A persistent myth; neuroimaging shows all brain regions have known functions and are active

claim: Quantum computers will break all current encryption within 5 years, making blockchain obsolete
notes: Current quantum computers are far from breaking RSA/ECC; post-quantum cryptography is being standardized

claim: The global EV market will reach 50% of new car sales by 2030
notes: IEA projects ~35-45% depending on policies; China already above 50% but other markets lag
